# Proposal: SQLite Fallback for node:sqlite Failures

**Issue:** #64695  
**Status:** Ready for Implementation  
**Priority:** P0

## Problem

OpenClaw crashes on startup when the native `node:sqlite` module fails to load. This occurs on:

- Systems without proper build tools
- Certain Node.js versions
- Docker containers without sqlite3 dev libraries

## Proposed Solution

Add graceful fallback to `better-sqlite3` when native module unavailable.

## Implementation

```typescript
// src/database/sqlite-backend.ts
import { DatabaseBackend } from "./types";
import * as nodeSqlite from "node:sqlite";

export class SqliteBackend implements DatabaseBackend {
  private db: any;
  private useNative: boolean = true;

  constructor(dbPath: string) {
    this.db = this.createConnection(dbPath);
  }

  private createConnection(dbPath: string): any {
    // Try native node:sqlite first
    try {
      if (nodeSqlite.DatabaseSync) {
        console.log("[sqlite] Using native node:sqlite module");
        this.useNative = true;
        return new nodeSqlite.DatabaseSync(dbPath);
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn("[sqlite] Native module unavailable:", error);
    }

    // Fallback to better-sqlite3
    try {
      const betterSqlite3 = require("better-sqlite3");
      console.log("[sqlite] Falling back to better-sqlite3");
      this.useNative = false;
      return betterSqlite3(dbPath);
    } catch (fallbackError) {
      throw new Error(
        `Failed to open database at ${dbPath}. ` +
          `Native error: ${error}, Fallback error: ${fallbackError}`,
      );
    }
  }

  getBackendType(): string {
    return this.useNative ? "node:sqlite" : "better-sqlite3";
  }

  exec(sql: string): void {
    this.db.exec(sql);
  }

  // ... rest of implementation
}
```

## Dependencies

Add to `package.json`:

```json
{
  "optionalDependencies": {
    "better-sqlite3": "^9.0.0"
  }
}
```

## Testing

```bash
# Test with native module
node -e "require('./dist/sqlite-backend.js')"

# Test fallback (simulate native failure)
NODE_SQLITE_SKIP=1 node -e "require('./dist/sqlite-backend.js')"
```

## Benefits

- ✅ Works on all systems
- ✅ No breaking changes
- ✅ Automatic fallback
- ✅ Debuggable (logs backend type)
